webhooks.name: vagentcard.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for AgentCard resources that ensures spec.targetRef is present and that no two AgentCards in the same namespace point to the same workload (apiVersion + kind + name), preventing duplicate agent registrations for a single backing workload.

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates create and update requests for ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources by enforcing model format priority uniqueness across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, ensuring consistent priorities within a single runtime, and validating multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (e.g., GPU types, parallel size bounds, disallowed env vars, container counts, and preventing removal of existing WorkerSpecs).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods owned by KServe InferenceServices on creation to inject storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential volumes/env vars (for accessing model storage), inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, Istio CNI security contexts, and optional modelcar (OCI image) init containers.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults LLMInferenceService resources by applying standard defaults and then matching the service's model URI against LocalModelCache (cluster-scoped) and LocalModelNamespaceCache (namespace-scoped) entries, setting labels and annotations to wire up local model PVC caching when a matching cache exists.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService resources on create and update by enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (mutual exclusivity of pipeline vs data parallelism, worker-parallelism co-requirements, positive values), scheduler config (replicas > 0, inline vs ref mutual exclusivity), workload scaling rules, and immutability of total parallelism size across updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults LLMInferenceService resources by applying standard defaults and then matching the service's model URI against LocalModelCache (cluster-scoped) and LocalModelNamespaceCache (namespace-scoped) entries, setting labels and annotations to wire up local model PVC caching when a matching cache exists.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources on create and update. Enforces business rules including router/gateway/HTTPRoute cross-field consistency, parallelism constraints (pipeline vs data parallelism mutual exclusivity, worker requires parallelism), immutability of total parallelism size on updates, scheduler config validity, LoRA adapter name uniqueness and safety, and autoscaling configuration (HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, replica bounds, WVA variantCost format, and actuator-backend consistency between decode and prefill workloads).

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting specs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify non-positive scheduler replicas, and emits warnings when well-known (built-in) configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by forbidding the baseRefs field (disabling recursive refs), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, running any injected validation function, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates create and update requests for ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources by enforcing model format priority uniqueness across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, ensuring consistent priorities within a single runtime, and validating multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (e.g., GPU types, parallel size bounds, disallowed env vars, container counts, and preventing removal of existing WorkerSpecs).

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to prevent model format priority conflicts between runtimes sharing the same protocol version, ensure priority consistency for the same model format within a single runtime, and enforce multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (parallel size bounds, single-container limit, GPU resource type validation, blocked environment variables).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods owned by KServe InferenceServices during creation to inject storage initializer containers (for model downloading with credentials), inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, Istio CNI security contexts, and optionally modelcar (OCI image source) support.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Defaults LLMInferenceService resources by reading the inference service config to check if local model caching is enabled, then matching the service's model URI against existing LocalModelCache and LocalModelNamespaceCache resources to set labels and annotations that link the service to the appropriate local model PVC (with namespace-scoped caches taking precedence over cluster-scoped ones).

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for LLMInferenceService (v1alpha1) that enforces structural and cross-field constraints on create and update: mutual exclusivity of HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec and managed vs user-defined gateway, parallelism rules (worker requires parallelism, pipeline and data parallelism are mutually exclusive, data/dataLocal must be paired, positive values), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), workload scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2), and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Defaults LLMInferenceService resources by reading the inference service config to check if local model caching is enabled, then matching the service's model URI against existing LocalModelCache and LocalModelNamespaceCache resources to set labels and annotations that link the service to the appropriate local model PVC (with namespace-scoped caches taking precedence over cluster-scoped ones).

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for the LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 custom resource that enforces structural and cross-field constraints on create and update: router/HTTPRoute consistency with gateway refs, parallelism configuration (pipeline vs data parallelism, immutable total size on update), scheduler config (inline vs ref, replicas), autoscaling (WVA with mutually exclusive HPA/KEDA backends, replica bounds, variantCost format, idle replica semantics, controller-owned field restrictions, actuator consistency across decode and prefill workloads), LoRA adapter uniqueness and naming, managed DRA annotation completeness and format, and confidential model spec validation.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify invalid scheduler replicas (<=0), and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting specs that include the forbidden baseRefs field or invalid scheduler replicas (must be >0), and emitting warnings when well-known (built-in) configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to prevent model format priority conflicts between runtimes sharing the same protocol version, ensure priority consistency for the same model format within a single runtime, and enforce multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (parallel size bounds, single-container limit, GPU resource type validation, blocked environment variables).

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates updates to Notebook resources, specifically preventing removal of the MLflow instance annotation while a notebook is running. This guards against a broken state where MLflow environment variables persist in the pod but the associated RoleBinding (granting MLflow permissions) is deleted immediately, leaving the MLflow client unable to authenticate.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that injects sidecar containers (kube-rbac-proxy for auth), mounts trusted CA certificate bundles, resolves container images from OpenShift ImageStreams, injects cluster-wide proxy environment variables, syncs and mounts pipeline runtime ConfigMaps and Elyra/DSPA pipeline configuration secrets, handles MLflow tracking URI injection, sets a reconciliation lock annotation on create, and blocks pod-template-changing mutations on running notebooks to prevent unintended restarts.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults RayCluster resources by detecting whether the cluster runs on OpenShift (via RESTMapper lookup for the Route CRD) and, if so, enforces secure trusted networking by setting an annotation and forcibly disabling Ingress/Route creation to ensure all access goes through Gateway API authentication.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults RayCluster resources by detecting whether the cluster runs on OpenShift (via RESTMapper lookup for the Route CRD) and, if so, enforces secure trusted networking by setting an annotation and forcibly disabling Ingress/Route creation to ensure all access goes through Gateway API authentication.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, ensuring the cluster name matches a DNS-compatible lowercase alphanumeric pattern and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook applies default values to ModelRegistry CRs (including migrating legacy OAuthProxy config to KubeRBACProxy), and validating webhook enforces spec correctness and cluster-wide name uniqueness on create by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources across namespaces.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook applies default values to ModelRegistry CRs (including migrating legacy OAuthProxy config to KubeRBACProxy), and validating webhook enforces spec correctness and cluster-wide name uniqueness on create by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources across namespaces.

webhooks.name: vaitenant.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that enforces AITenant resources are only created in the configured infrastructure namespace. It rejects any AITenant whose namespace does not match the preconfigured AITenantNamespace, ensuring all tenants are co-located in the designated control-plane namespace.

webhooks.name: vmaasauthpolicy.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for MaaSAuthPolicy creation that ensures the resource is only created in namespaces where a Tenant CR already exists, enforcing proper tenant initialization before auth policies can be defined.

webhooks.name: vmaassubscription.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that rejects creation of MaaSSubscription resources in namespaces that have not been initialized as MaaS tenants. On create it lists Tenant CRs in the target namespace and denies admission if none exist, ensuring proper tenant setup before subscriptions can be created.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating (defaulting) and validating webhooks for KServe InferenceGraph v1alpha1 resources. The validator enforces that serviceURL references in graph node steps use only cluster-internal hostnames and target services within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security reasons (e.g., preventing Authorization header leaks).

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates and defaults KServe InferenceService resources. The validating webhook rejects creation of InferenceServices in the protected application namespace (determined by reading DSCInitialization) and enforces name-length constraints. The mutating webhook is a no-op placeholder that currently applies no defaults.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming Pods that have RAY_USE_TLS enabled on their kserve-container or worker-container by injecting TLS-related volumes, volume mounts, and an init container that copies Ray TLS certificates from a shared Secret into the Pod, enabling mTLS communication between Ray cluster nodes used for distributed inference serving.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates and defaults KServe InferenceService resources. The validating webhook rejects creation of InferenceServices in the protected application namespace (determined by reading DSCInitialization) and enforces name-length constraints. The mutating webhook is a no-op placeholder that currently applies no defaults.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation by enforcing a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. On create, it lists existing Accounts in the target namespace and rejects the request if one already exists; updates and deletes are always allowed.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating (defaulting) and validating webhooks for KServe InferenceGraph v1alpha1 resources. The validator enforces that serviceURL references in graph node steps use only cluster-internal hostnames and target services within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security reasons (e.g., preventing Authorization header leaks).

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for OGXServer resources on create and update. It rejects unknown distribution names (checked against the operator's embedded registry), duplicate provider IDs, model-to-provider references that point to non-existent providers, and adoption annotations whose value equals the CR name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts). It also emits deprecation warnings for renamed distributions (e.g. rh-dev → rh).

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces and enriches them with configuration from their parent SparkApplication CR—including volumes, environment variables, config maps, ports, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, security contexts, GPU resources, sidecars, init containers, owner references, memory limits, and other scheduling/runtime settings for both driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources during create and update operations. It only applies defaults when the application is in 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, delegating to the operator's scheme-registered defaulting logic (operatorscheme.WebhookScheme.Default) to populate unset fields with sensible values.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update, ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label so that downstream Kubernetes Services and SparkApplication resources derived from it will have compliant names. Additional validation logic is stubbed but not yet implemented.

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: A mutating admission webhook defaulter for ScheduledSparkApplication resources. Currently it is a no-op stub that logs when a ScheduledSparkApplication is being mutated but does not yet set any default values on the resource.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that processes the 'opendatahub.io/connections' annotation: validates that referenced secrets exist and the requesting user has 'get' permission on them (via SubjectAccessReview), then injects or removes those secrets as envFrom entries in the notebook's container spec on create/update operations.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService resources that automatically injects data-connection details (model URI, image pull secrets, service account) based on a connection annotation. It supports URI/HuggingFace, S3, and OCI connection types, reading the referenced Secret to build the appropriate spec fields, and handles cleanup and replacement when the connection annotation changes.

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects model-serving connection details (storage URIs, S3 storage keys, OCI image pull secrets, service account names) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation, and cleans up or replaces those injected fields when the annotation is updated or removed.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: MonitoringServiceName component enabled OR MonitoringServiceName component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that automatically injects the monitoring.opendatahub.io/scrape=true label onto PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources created or updated in namespaces labeled for ODH monitoring, after verifying that the platform Monitoring CR exists (i.e., monitoring is enabled).

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile settings (resource requests/limits, node selectors, tolerations) from a referenced HardwareProfile CR specified via annotations, handling profile switching by clearing old settings before applying new ones and profile removal by cleaning up previously injected configuration.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create/update. Specifically, it defaults ModelRegistry.RegistriesNamespace to the operator's standard namespace when ModelRegistry is Managed and the field is empty, and defaults Kserve.NIM.ManagementState to Managed when Kserve is Managed and the NIM state is unset.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 resources on create and update. On create, enforces a singleton constraint (denies if another DataScienceCluster already exists) and rejects Kueue with ManagementState set to Managed. On update, only rejects Kueue Managed state. All other operations (including delete) are allowed unconditionally.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v2 create and delete operations. On create, it enforces a singleton constraint by denying creation if a DSCInitialization instance already exists. On delete, it prevents removal of the DSCInitialization while any DataScienceCluster resource still exists, ensuring the initialization resource outlives all clusters that depend on it.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create/update. Specifically, it defaults ModelRegistry.RegistriesNamespace to the operator's default namespace when ModelRegistry is Managed and the field is empty, and defaults Kserve.NIM.ManagementState to Managed when Kserve is Managed and NIM's state is unset.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 admission requests: on create, enforces a singleton constraint (only one DSC allowed) and denies Kueue with ManagementState Managed; on update, denies Kueue Managed state and prevents v1 API updates when v2-only components are currently Managed to avoid silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v1 resources as a singleton: on create, denies the request if another DSCInitialization instance already exists; on delete, denies the request if any DataScienceCluster instance still exists, preventing removal of the initialization resource while the platform is in use.

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for Workspace resources (kubeflow.org/v1beta1) that ensures on create and update the referenced WorkspaceKind exists and the user-selected imageConfig and podConfig IDs are valid options defined in that WorkspaceKind, while also validating pod template metadata labels and annotations.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for WorkspaceKind resources (kubeflow.org/v1beta1) that enforces structural correctness on create (imageConfig/podConfig values, ports, redirects, environment variables, pod metadata labels/annotations, default config references), validates only changed fields on update while lazily querying Workspace usage counts to prevent removal of in-use config options, and blocks deletion when Workspaces still reference the WorkspaceKind or a protection finalizer is present.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist as LocalModelNodeGroup resources. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService or LLMInferenceService still references the cache via label matching.

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, enforcing naming conventions (DNS-1035), run policy constraints, elastic policy configuration rules (e.g., nProcPerNode deprecation), valid replica types (Master/Worker), required container images, the presence of a default 'pytorch' container, and correct replica counts (exactly 1 for Master, at least 1 for Worker).

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, replica specs contain only valid types (Master/Worker), exactly one Master replica exists, every container has an image, and at least one container is named 'xgboost'.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, replica specs are present with properly configured containers (each having an image and at least one container named 'tensorflow'), and that no more than one Chief or Master replica role is defined.

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that jaxReplicaSpecs are present with only the 'Worker' replica type, that each replica spec has containers with images defined, and that at least one container is named 'jax'.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, replica types are limited to Master and Worker, each replica spec has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. On updates it also validates RunPolicy immutability constraints.

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by checking that the replicated jobs in the runtime spec are well-formed, and warns on create if the runtime is marked as deprecated via labels.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the referenced runtime from a registry and delegating to that runtime's ValidateObjects method, rejecting requests that reference an unsupported runtime or fail runtime-specific validation rules.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on create, ensuring that each ReplicatedJob tagged with a known ancestor label (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) has exactly 1 replica and contains the required container name for that ancestor. Update and delete are no-ops.

webhooks.name: vagentruntime.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for AgentRuntime resources that enforces two invariants on create/update: (1) no two AgentRuntimes in the same namespace may target the same workload (apiVersion+kind+name), preventing conflicting sidecar configurations, and (2) tlsBridgeMode=enabled is only allowed with authBridgeMode proxy-sidecar or lite, since the TLS bridge runs in the Go forward proxy which does not exist in other deployment shapes.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced NodeGroups exist; on delete, it prevents removal if the cache is still referenced by any InferenceService or LLMInferenceService via local model labels.

webhooks.name: conversion.dscinitializations.dscinitialization.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Implements CRD conversion webhook for DSCInitialization, converting between the v1 (spoke) and v2 (hub) API versions. It maps spec fields (ApplicationsNamespace, Monitoring, TrustedCABundle, DevFlags) and status fields bidirectionally so that clients using either API version see a consistent representation of the DSCInitialization singleton.
